title: mentha pulegium var. pulegioides
intro: Mentha pulegium var. pulegioides, also known as water mint or pennycress mint, is a variety of the peppermint plant (Mentha pulegium) that has been traditionally used in herbal medicine. It is valued for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and carminative properties, which make it effective in treating digestive issues, skin problems, and respiratory infections. The health benefits of Mentha pulegium var. pulegioides include relief from nausea, headaches, and fever, as well as improved digestion and reduced inflammation. The plant can be used to make teas, infusions, and topical preparations, such as salves and creams, that can be applied to the skin or ingested for medicinal purposes.
